duplicate_of.matched_title: attention sentiment surge in google trends for debt consolidation searches

idea.title: Attention Sentiment Weekly Surge In Google Trends For Bankruptcy Searches
idea.thesis: Spike in bankruptcy searches signals rising financial distress, bearish for financial sector. Financial distress indicators often predict financial sector pressure.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search interest for bankruptcy
idea.signal_logic: Enter short XLF if bankruptcy searches rise >25% WoW for 2 consecutive weeks
idea.entry_exit: Entry: Enter short XLF if bankruptcy searches rise >25% WoW for 2 consecutive weeks Exit: Exit after searches drop below 15% WoW increase or after 6 weeks
idea.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
idea.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
idea.implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest for bankruptcy via api (weekly).
idea.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Bankruptcy search spikes often precede or coincide with financial sector volatility.

idea.title: Consumer Stress Weekly Google Trends Spike In Unemployment Benefits Searches
idea.thesis: Spike in unemployment benefits searches signals rising labor stress, bearish for consumer discretionary. Rising unemployment concerns reduce consumer spending power.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'unemployment benefits'
idea.signal_logic: Enter short XLY if searches rise >20% WoW for 2 weeks
idea.entry_exit: Entry: Enter short XLY if searches rise >20% WoW for 2 weeks Exit: Exit after searches normalize below 10% WoW increase or after 6 weeks
idea.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
idea.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
idea.implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest for 'unemployment benefits' via api (weekly).
idea.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Unemployment-related search interest spikes frequently during economic uncertainty.
